
### Effective Number of Topics per Publication

```{r effective-topics, fig.width=10, fig.height=5}
# Publication-level topic distribution (how many topics does each pub actually "use"?)
pub_topic_dist <- topic_props %>%
  group_by(corpus) %>%
  summarize(across(starts_with("Topic"), mean), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_longer(-corpus, names_to = "topic", values_to = "prevalence") %>%
  mutate(
    topic_num = as.integer(str_remove(topic, "Topic")),
    publication = ifelse(corpus == "Womans_Exponent",
                         "Woman's Exponent", "Relief Society Magazine"),
    label = str_trunc(str_remove(topic_labels[topic_num], "^Topic \\d+: "), 25)
  )

# Effective number of topics = exp(entropy of topic distribution)
pub_ent <- pub_topic_dist %>%
  filter(prevalence > 0) %>%
  group_by(publication) %>%
  summarize(
    eff_topics = exp(-sum(prevalence * log(prevalence))),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
cat("\nEffective number of topics:\n")
for (i in 1:nrow(pub_ent)) {
  cat(sprintf("  %s: %.1f (of %d)\n", pub_ent$publication[i], pub_ent$eff_topics[i], chosen_K))
}

# Stacked area showing topic weight per publication
ggplot(pub_topic_dist, aes(x = reorder(label, -prevalence), y = prevalence,
                            fill = publication)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Woman's Exponent" = "#CD661D",
                               "Relief Society Magazine" = "#458B74")) +
  labs(title = "Topic Weight Distribution by Publication",
       subtitle = paste0("Effective topics: WE = ", round(pub_ent$eff_topics[pub_ent$publication == "Woman's Exponent"], 1),
                         " | RSM = ", round(pub_ent$eff_topics[pub_ent$publication == "Relief Society Magazine"], 1)),
       x = NULL, y = "Mean Proportion", fill = "Publication") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
        legend.position = "bottom") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format())
```

---

## 25. Temporal Trajectory Analysis

How do topic prevalences evolve within each publication, and what happens at the transition point?

```{r temporal-trajectory, fig.width=14, fig.height=10}
cat(rep("=", 70), "\n", sep = "")
cat("TEMPORAL TRAJECTORY ANALYSIS\n")
cat(rep("=", 70), "\n\n", sep = "")

# Yearly topic prevalence per publication
yearly_topics <- topic_props %>%
  filter(!is.na(year)) %>%
  select(year, corpus, starts_with("Topic")) %>%
  group_by(year, corpus) %>%
  summarize(across(starts_with("Topic"), mean), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_longer(starts_with("Topic"), names_to = "topic", values_to = "prevalence") %>%
  mutate(
    topic_num = as.integer(str_remove(topic, "Topic")),
    publication = ifelse(corpus == "Womans_Exponent",
                         "Woman's Exponent", "Relief Society Magazine"),
    label = str_remove(topic_labels[topic_num], "^Topic \\d+: ")
  )

# Pick top 6 most significant topics
top_sig_topics <- corpus_effects %>%
  arrange(P_value) %>%
  head(6) %>%
  pull(Topic)

yearly_topics %>%
  filter(topic_num %in% top_sig_topics) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = prevalence, color = publication)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2, linewidth = 1.2) +
  facet_wrap(~str_wrap(label, 30), ncol = 3, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Woman's Exponent" = "#CD661D",
                                "Relief Society Magazine" = "#458B74")) +
  labs(title = "Topic Trajectories Over Time",
       subtitle = "Top 6 most significantly different topics with LOESS trends",
       x = "Year", y = "Mean Prevalence", color = "Publication") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format())
```

### Shared Topic Trajectories

```{r shared-trajectories, fig.width=14, fig.height=10}
# Topics with LEAST difference = most shared
shared_traj_topics <- corpus_effects %>%
  arrange(abs(Difference_Pct)) %>%
  head(6) %>%
  pull(Topic)

yearly_topics %>%
  filter(topic_num %in% shared_traj_topics) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = prevalence, color = publication)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2, linewidth = 1.2) +
  facet_wrap(~str_wrap(label, 30), ncol = 3, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Woman's Exponent" = "#CD661D",
                                "Relief Society Magazine" = "#458B74")) +
  labs(title = "Shared Topic Trajectories",
       subtitle = "Topics with LEAST difference between publications — continuity in discourse",
       x = "Year", y = "Mean Prevalence", color = "Publication") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format())
```

### Topic Prevalence Heatmap Over Time

```{r topic-time-heatmap, fig.width=14, fig.height=8}
# Heatmap: years × topics, faceted by publication
yearly_topics %>%
  mutate(short_label = paste0("T", topic_num)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = short_label, fill = prevalence)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.3) +
  facet_wrap(~publication, ncol = 2) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "#2E4A1E",
                      labels = percent_format()) +
  labs(title = "Topic Prevalence Over Time: Full Landscape",
       subtitle = "Darker = higher prevalence in that year",
       x = "Year", y = "Topic", fill = "Prevalence") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
        panel.grid = element_blank()) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev)
```

---

### Percentage Change in Topics Between Publications

How much does each topic's prevalence **shift** from the Exponent era to the Magazine era? This captures the editorial transition in quantitative terms.

```{r topic-pct-change, fig.width=12, fig.height=8}
cat(rep("=", 70), "\n", sep = "")
cat("PERCENTAGE CHANGE IN TOPICS: WE -> RSM\n")
cat(rep("=", 70), "\n\n", sep = "")

# Mean prevalence per topic per publication
era_means <- topic_props %>%
  group_by(corpus) %>%
  summarize(across(starts_with("Topic"), mean), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_longer(-corpus, names_to = "topic", values_to = "prevalence") %>%
  mutate(topic_num = as.integer(str_remove(topic, "Topic"))) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = corpus, values_from = prevalence)

# Compute percentage change: (RSM - WE) / WE * 100
era_change <- era_means %>%
  mutate(
    label = str_remove(topic_labels[topic_num], "^Topic \\d+: "),
    we_pct = Womans_Exponent * 100,
    rsm_pct = Relief_Society_Magazine * 100,
    abs_change = rsm_pct - we_pct,
    pct_change = ifelse(Womans_Exponent > 0.001,
                        (Relief_Society_Magazine - Womans_Exponent) / Womans_Exponent * 100,
                        NA_real_),
    direction = case_when(
      abs_change > 0.5  ~ "Grew in Magazine",
      abs_change < -0.5 ~ "Declined in Magazine",
      TRUE ~ "Stable"
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs_change))

cat("Topic changes (WE era -> RSM era):\n\n")
cat(sprintf("%-35s  %6s  %6s  %8s  %8s\n",
            "Topic", "WE %", "RSM %", "Change", "% Change"))
cat(rep("-", 80), "\n", sep = "")
for (i in 1:nrow(era_change)) {
  r <- era_change[i, ]
  pct_str <- ifelse(is.na(r$pct_change), "N/A", sprintf("%+.0f%%", r$pct_change))
  cat(sprintf("%-35s  %5.1f%%  %5.1f%%  %+6.1fpp  %8s\n",
              str_trunc(r$label, 35), r$we_pct, r$rsm_pct, r$abs_change, pct_str))
}

# --- Absolute change bar chart (percentage points) ---
p_abs <- era_change %>%
  mutate(display = paste0("T", topic_num, ": ", str_trunc(label, 28)),
         display = reorder(display, abs_change)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = abs_change, y = display, fill = direction)) +
  geom_col(alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linewidth = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Grew in Magazine" = "#458B74",
                                "Declined in Magazine" = "#CD661D",
                                "Stable" = "#94a3b8")) +
  labs(title = "Topic Change: Woman's Exponent -> Relief Society Magazine",
       subtitle = "Absolute change in percentage points of topic prevalence",
       x = "Change (percentage points)", y = NULL, fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", panel.grid.major.y = element_blank())

print(p_abs)
```

```{r topic-pct-change-relative, fig.width=12, fig.height=8}
# --- Relative (%) change bar chart ---
era_change_filtered <- era_change %>%
  filter(!is.na(pct_change), abs(pct_change) < 500) %>%  # Cap extreme ratios
  mutate(display = paste0("T", topic_num, ": ", str_trunc(label, 28)),
         display = reorder(display, pct_change))

if (nrow(era_change_filtered) > 0) {
  ggplot(era_change_filtered, aes(x = pct_change, y = display, fill = direction)) +
    geom_col(alpha = 0.85) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linewidth = 1) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Grew in Magazine" = "#458B74",
                                  "Declined in Magazine" = "#CD661D",
                                  "Stable" = "#94a3b8")) +
    labs(title = "Relative Percentage Change in Topic Prevalence",
         subtitle = "How much did each topic grow or shrink relative to its Exponent-era baseline?",
         x = "Relative Change (%)", y = NULL, fill = NULL) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom", panel.grid.major.y = element_blank())
}
```

### Topic Change Over Time (Year-by-Year Gap)

```{r topic-gap-over-time, fig.width=14, fig.height=10}
# For years where BOTH publications have data, compute the prevalence gap
yearly_gap <- yearly_topics %>%
  select(year, corpus, topic_num, prevalence, label) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = corpus, values_from = prevalence) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Womans_Exponent), !is.na(Relief_Society_Magazine)) %>%
  mutate(gap = Relief_Society_Magazine - Womans_Exponent)

# If there's temporal overlap, show the gap over time
if (nrow(yearly_gap) > 0) {
  # Top 6 topics with largest variance in gap
  gap_var <- yearly_gap %>%
    group_by(topic_num, label) %>%
    summarize(gap_var = var(gap, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    arrange(desc(gap_var)) %>%
    head(6)

  yearly_gap %>%
    filter(topic_num %in% gap_var$topic_num) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = year, y = gap, fill = gap > 0)) +
    geom_col(alpha = 0.7, show.legend = FALSE) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.8) +
    facet_wrap(~str_wrap(label, 25), ncol = 3, scales = "free_y") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "#458B74", "FALSE" = "#CD661D")) +
    labs(title = "Year-by-Year Topic Prevalence Gap (RSM - WE)",
         subtitle = "Green = more prevalent in RSM that year | Orange = more prevalent in WE",
         x = "Year", y = "Prevalence Gap (RSM - WE)") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
    theme(strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9)) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format())
} else {
  cat("No overlapping years between publications — gap analysis requires temporal overlap.\n")
  cat("Showing era-to-era comparison instead (see absolute change chart above).\n")
}
```

---

## 26. Complementary Evolution Summary

```{r complementary-summary, fig.width=12, fig.height=8}
cat(rep("=", 70), "\n", sep = "")
cat("COMPLEMENTARY EVOLUTION SUMMARY\n")
cat(rep("=", 70), "\n\n", sep = "")

# Build a comprehensive topic classification
topic_class <- pub_means %>%
  left_join(corpus_effects %>% select(Topic, Significant, Difference_Pct, P_value),
            by = c("topic_num" = "Topic")) %>%
  mutate(
    evolution_type = case_when(
      !Significant ~ "Stable Core",
      category == "WE Signature" ~ "WE Distinctive",
      category == "RSM Signature" ~ "RSM Distinctive",
      TRUE ~ "Shifting Emphasis"
    ),
    evolution_type = factor(evolution_type,
                            levels = c("Stable Core", "Shifting Emphasis",
                                       "WE Distinctive", "RSM Distinctive"))
  )

cat("COMPLEMENTARY EVOLUTION CLASSIFICATION:\n\n")

cat("STABLE CORE — topics sustained across both publications:\n")
stable <- topic_class %>% filter(evolution_type == "Stable Core")
if (nrow(stable) > 0) {
  for (i in 1:nrow(stable)) {
    cat(sprintf("  T%d: %s (WE: %.1f%%, RSM: %.1f%%)\n",
                stable$topic_num[i], stable$label[i],
                stable$we_pct[i], stable$rsm_pct[i]))
  }
} else cat("  (none)\n")

cat("\nSHIFTING EMPHASIS — present in both but weighted differently:\n")
shifting <- topic_class %>% filter(evolution_type == "Shifting Emphasis")
if (nrow(shifting) > 0) {
  for (i in 1:nrow(shifting)) {
    cat(sprintf("  T%d: %s (WE: %.1f%%, RSM: %.1f%%, p=%.3f)\n",
                shifting$topic_num[i], shifting$label[i],
                shifting$we_pct[i], shifting$rsm_pct[i], shifting$P_value[i]))
  }
} else cat("  (none)\n")

cat("\nWE DISTINCTIVE — topics much more prevalent in the Exponent:\n")
we_dist <- topic_class %>% filter(evolution_type == "WE Distinctive")
if (nrow(we_dist) > 0) {
  for (i in 1:nrow(we_dist)) {
    cat(sprintf("  T%d: %s (WE: %.1f%% vs RSM: %.1f%%)\n",
                we_dist$topic_num[i], we_dist$label[i],
                we_dist$we_pct[i], we_dist$rsm_pct[i]))
  }
} else cat("  (none)\n")

cat("\nRSM DISTINCTIVE — topics much more prevalent in the Magazine:\n")
rsm_dist <- topic_class %>% filter(evolution_type == "RSM Distinctive")
if (nrow(rsm_dist) > 0) {
  for (i in 1:nrow(rsm_dist)) {
    cat(sprintf("  T%d: %s (RSM: %.1f%% vs WE: %.1f%%)\n",
                rsm_dist$topic_num[i], rsm_dist$label[i],
                rsm_dist$rsm_pct[i], rsm_dist$we_pct[i]))
  }
} else cat("  (none)\n")

# Visual summary
ggplot(topic_class, aes(x = we_pct, y = rsm_pct, color = evolution_type)) +
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey70", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 5, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = paste0("T", topic_num, ": ", str_trunc(label, 25))),
                  size = 3, max.overlaps = 20, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "Stable Core" = "#6495ED",
    "Shifting Emphasis" = "#DAA520",
    "WE Distinctive" = "#CD661D",
    "RSM Distinctive" = "#458B74"
  )) +
  labs(title = "Complementary Evolution of Topics",
       subtitle = "Diagonal = equal prevalence | Distance from line = degree of difference",
       x = "Woman's Exponent (Mean %)", y = "Relief Society Magazine (Mean %)",
       color = "Evolution Type") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16)) +
  coord_fixed()
```

---

## 27. Export Results

```{r export-results}
cat("Exporting results to:", output_path, "\n\n")

# 1. Publication comparison table
write_csv(corpus_effects,
          file.path(output_path, paste0("publication_comparison_K", chosen_K, ".csv")))
cat("  publication_comparison.csv\n")

# 2. Significant differences
if (nrow(significant_topics) > 0) {
  write_csv(significant_topics,
            file.path(output_path, paste0("significant_differences_K", chosen_K, ".csv")))
  cat("  significant_differences.csv\n")
}

# 3. Topic summary with words
topic_props_mean <- colMeans(topic_props %>% select(starts_with("Topic")))
write_csv(tibble(
    topic_num = 1:chosen_K,
    label = str_remove(topic_labels, "^Topic \\d+: "),
    prevalence_pct = round(topic_props_mean * 100, 2),
    frex_words = apply(all_top_words$frex, 1, function(x) paste(x, collapse = ", ")),
    prob_words = apply(all_top_words$prob, 1, function(x) paste(x, collapse = ", "))
  ),
  file.path(output_path, paste0("topic_summary_K", chosen_K, ".csv")))
cat("  topic_summary.csv\n")

# 4. Full topic-article link
write_csv(topic_article_link %>% mutate(proportion_pct = round(proportion * 100, 2)),
          file.path(output_path, paste0("topic_article_full_link_K", chosen_K, ".csv")))
cat("  topic_article_full_link.csv\n")

# 5. Dominant topic per article
write_csv(dominant_topics,
          file.path(output_path, paste0("dominant_topic_per_article_K", chosen_K, ".csv")))
cat("  dominant_topic_per_article.csv\n")

# 6. Strong exemplars only
write_csv(dominant_topics %>% filter(strong_exemplar),
          file.path(output_path, paste0("strong_exemplars_K", chosen_K, ".csv")))
cat("  strong_exemplars.csv\n")

# 7. Top 25 exemplar articles per topic
write_csv(topic_article_link %>%
            group_by(topic_num, topic_label) %>%
            slice_max(proportion, n = 25) %>%
            ungroup() %>%
            mutate(proportion_pct = round(proportion * 100, 2)),
          file.path(output_path, paste0("topic_exemplar_articles_K", chosen_K, ".csv")))
cat("  topic_exemplar_articles.csv\n")

# 8. Topic classification (continuity analysis)
if (exists("topic_class")) {
  write_csv(topic_class %>% select(topic_num, label, category, evolution_type,
                                    we_pct, rsm_pct, Significant, Difference_Pct, P_value),
            file.path(output_path, paste0("topic_classification_K", chosen_K, ".csv")))
  cat("  topic_classification.csv\n")
}

cat("\nAll results exported.\n")
```

---

## 28. Summary Statistics

```{r summary-stats}
cat("\n", rep("=", 70), "\n", sep = "")
cat("COMPARISON SUMMARY\n")
cat(rep("=", 70), "\n\n", sep = "")

cat("Publications Compared:\n")
cat("  Woman's Exponent (", sum(meta$corpus == "Womans_Exponent"), " docs)\n", sep = "")
cat("  Relief Society Magazine (", sum(meta$corpus == "Relief_Society_Magazine"), " docs)\n\n", sep = "")

cat("Topic Analysis:\n")
cat("  Total topics:", chosen_K, "\n")
cat("  Significant differences:", sum(corpus_effects$Significant), "\n")
cat("    More in Woman's Exponent:", sum(corpus_effects$Direction == "More in Woman's Exponent"), "\n")
cat("    More in RS Magazine:", sum(corpus_effects$Direction == "More in Relief Society Magazine"), "\n")
cat("    No difference:", sum(corpus_effects$Direction == "No significant difference"), "\n\n")

cat("Largest differences:\n")
top_3 <- corpus_effects %>% head(3)
for (i in 1:nrow(top_3)) {
  cat(sprintf("  %s: %.2f%% (%s)\n", top_3$Label[i], top_3$Difference_Pct[i], top_3$Direction[i]))
}
```

---

## 29. Author-Topic Heatmap

```{r author-topic-heatmap, fig.width=12, fig.height=10}
cat(rep("=", 70), "\n", sep = "")
cat("AUTHOR-TOPIC ANALYSIS\n")
cat(rep("=", 70), "\n\n", sep = "")

# Build author-topic data from document-topic proportions
author_topic_data <- topic_props %>%
  filter(!is.na(Author), Author != "", nchar(Author) > 1) %>%
  mutate(Author = str_trim(Author))

if (nrow(author_topic_data) >= 10) {
  # Get top 25 most frequent authors
  top_authors <- author_topic_data %>%
    count(Author, sort = TRUE) %>%
    head(25) %>%
    pull(Author)

  # Calculate mean topic proportion per author
  author_matrix_data <- author_topic_data %>%
    filter(Author %in% top_authors) %>%
    select(Author, starts_with("Topic")) %>%
    group_by(Author) %>%
    summarize(across(starts_with("Topic"), mean), .groups = "drop")

  mat <- as.matrix(author_matrix_data %>% select(-Author))
  rownames(mat) <- author_matrix_data$Author
  colnames(mat) <- paste0("T", 1:chosen_K, ": ",
                           str_trunc(str_remove(topic_labels, "^Topic \\d+: "), 18))

  # Replace any NaN/Inf
  mat[!is.finite(mat)] <- 0

  pheatmap(mat,
    main = paste0("Author-Topic Affinity (Top ", length(top_authors), " Authors)"),
    color = colorRampPalette(c("white", "#D4E8D4", "#6E8B3D", "#2E4A1E"))(100),
    cluster_rows = TRUE, cluster_cols = FALSE,
    fontsize_row = 8, fontsize_col = 8, angle_col = 45, border_color = "grey90")
} else {
  cat("Skipping author-topic heatmap: not enough named authors in the combined corpus.\n")
  cat("Articles with author data:", nrow(author_topic_data), "\n")
}
```

---

## 30. Author-Topic by Publication

```{r author-topic-by-pub, fig.width=14, fig.height=8}
if (exists("author_topic_data") && nrow(author_topic_data) >= 10) {

  # Top 10 authors per publication
  top_by_pub <- author_topic_data %>%
    count(Author, corpus, sort = TRUE) %>%
    group_by(corpus) %>%
    slice_head(n = 10) %>%
    pull(Author) %>%
    unique()

  pub_author_data <- author_topic_data %>%
    filter(Author %in% top_by_pub) %>%
    select(Author, corpus, starts_with("Topic")) %>%
    pivot_longer(starts_with("Topic"), names_to = "topic", values_to = "proportion") %>%
    mutate(topic_num = as.numeric(str_remove(topic, "Topic"))) %>%
    group_by(Author, corpus, topic_num) %>%
    summarize(mean_prop = mean(proportion), .groups = "drop") %>%
    mutate(
      topic_label = str_trunc(str_remove(topic_labels[topic_num], "^Topic \\d+: "), 20),
      publication = ifelse(corpus == "Relief_Society_Magazine",
                           "Relief Society Magazine", "Woman's Exponent")
    )

  # Top 3 topics per author
  top_topics_per_author <- pub_author_data %>%
    group_by(Author) %>%
    slice_max(mean_prop, n = 3) %>%
    ungroup()

  ggplot(top_topics_per_author,
         aes(x = reorder(Author, mean_prop), y = mean_prop,
             fill = topic_label)) +
    geom_col(position = "stack") +
    coord_flip() +
    facet_wrap(~publication, scales = "free_y") +
    scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set3") +
    labs(title = "Top 3 Topics per Author (by Publication)",
         x = NULL, y = "Mean Topic Proportion", fill = "Topic") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom",
          strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12)) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format())
}
```

---

## 31. Article Type Distribution

```{r article-type-dist, fig.width=12, fig.height=6}
type_data <- topic_props %>%
  filter(!is.na(Article_Type), Article_Type != "Unknown") %>%
  count(corpus, Article_Type, sort = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(publication = ifelse(corpus == "Relief_Society_Magazine",
                              "Relief Society Magazine", "Woman's Exponent"))

# Top 10 types per pub
top_types <- type_data %>%
  group_by(publication) %>%
  slice_max(n, n = 10) %>%
  pull(Article_Type) %>%
  unique()

type_data %>%
  filter(Article_Type %in% top_types) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(Article_Type, n), y = n, fill = publication)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Woman's Exponent" = "#CD661D",
                               "Relief Society Magazine" = "#458B74")) +
  labs(title = "Article Type Distribution by Publication",
       x = NULL, y = "Count", fill = "Publication") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

---

## Session Info

```{r session-info}
sessionInfo()
```
